text:
We have 100 bulbs, initially off.  
On round \( k \), we toggle bulbs at positions that are multiples of \( k \).

A bulb ends up on if it is toggled an odd number of times.  
The number of toggles for bulb \( n \) is the number of divisors of \( n \) (since each divisor \( k \) toggles it).

Only perfect squares have an odd number of divisors.  
So bulbs at positions \( 1^2, 2^2, \dots, 10^2 \) (since \( 10^2 = 100 \)) will be on.

That’s 10 bulbs.

Final answer:  
10
